source_key: A.1 Why turning deserves its own chapter
title: Why turning deserves its own chapter
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
role: Elevate turning to equal dignity with moving and resizing.
prompt_core: Create a compelling bridge image where translation arrows and scaling rays from earlier chapters are joined by a new protagonist: a clean rotating wheel or arm sweeping through angle. The composition should announce that turning is not a side note but another basic mathematical action with its own invariants. Celestial-mechanical mood, serious, elegant, slightly dramatic.
caption: Moving and resizing were never the whole story; turning completes the trio.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.
negative_block: No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.
api_prompt:
Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

Project context:
This image belongs to a rigorous adult mathematics book for neurodivergent visual thinkers.
It should function as a cognitive bridge for subsection "A.1 Why turning deserves its own chapter".
Treat it as editorial art with pedagogical duty.

Image role:
Elevate turning to equal dignity with moving and resizing.

Layout intent:
B — Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image

Style direction:
turning-opens-world plate

Core image brief:
Create a compelling bridge image where translation arrows and scaling rays from earlier chapters are joined by a new protagonist: a clean rotating wheel or arm sweeping through angle. The composition should announce that turning is not a side note but another basic mathematical action with its own invariants. Celestial-mechanical mood, serious, elegant, slightly dramatic.

Caption intent for layout reference only:
Moving and resizing were never the whole story; turning completes the trio.

Overlay / production note:
No mandatory overlay note.

Rendering instructions:
Do not place explanatory typography, equations, or labels inside the art unless they are intrinsic physical objects in the scene.
Preserve clear compositional space for later book-layout overlays where appropriate.
Make the concept immediately graspable to an intelligent adult who distrusts rote schooling and learns through spatial pattern, tactile intuition, and elegant structure.
Keep the mood intellectually serious, lucid, and memorable rather than cute.

Negative constraints:
No classroom clip art, no chalkboard wallpaper, no random floating equations, no childish mascots, no busy collage, no gibberish text, no impossible geometry, no decorative sci-fi fog, no glossy stock-photo students. If exact labels, axes, or theorem marks are needed, leave clean space for them to be overlaid later.

source_key: A.2 Angle is amount of turn, not just amount of corner
title: Angle is amount of turn, not just amount of corner
role: Break the frozen-school-diagram idea of angle as merely a corner shape.
prompt_core: Show a rigid ray or arm rotating from one direction to another, leaving a luminous swept arc behind it. A corner-like static picture can be faintly present in the background, but the foreground must insist that angle is an amount of turning. Dynamic yet clean, like a precision instrument caught in motion. No text.
caption: An angle measures a turn, not merely a shape at a corner.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. The image must clarify the concept rather than merely decorate it. Favor spatial legibility, tactile materials, intellectually serious editorial art direction, and one dominant idea per frame. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

source_key: A.3 Radians: the honest unit of angle
title: Radians: the honest unit of angle
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Make the arc-length definition of radian intuitive and elegant.
prompt_core: Illustrate a circle where one radius is lifted and then laid along the circumference, showing that the angle subtended by an arc equal in length to the radius is one natural unit of turn. The image should feel honest and inevitable, not arbitrary like degrees. Clean circle geometry, refined composition, optional overlay for arc and radius markings.
caption: A radian is built from the circle itself, not imposed from outside.
overlay: Overlay arc and radius marks after generation.

source_key: A.4 Sine, cosine, and tangent are shape fingerprints
title: Sine, cosine, and tangent are shape fingerprints
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
role: Turn trig functions into living geometric relationships instead of SOHCAHTOA chants.
prompt_core: Create a beautiful hybrid image of a point moving around a circle while vertical and horizontal projections drop to form sine and cosine, and a tangent line reveals tangent as a slope-like ratio. The motion around the circle should feel continuous and bodily. Keep the geometry clean enough for later labels, but rich enough to be memorable as art.
caption: Trig functions are projections and shape relations made visible.
overlay: Exact axes, circle, and projection lines should be overlaid later.

source_key: A.5 Another lens: shadows, slopes, and the unit circle
title: Another lens: shadows, slopes, and the unit circle
role: Offer multiple entrances into trig for different cognitive styles.
prompt_core: Make a triptych linking a sundial shadow, a hillside or roof slope, and the unit circle projection picture. The same turning quantity should quietly govern all three panels. The goal is cognitive generosity: whichever doorway the learner uses, the underlying structure is the same. Celestial light, clear composition, adult sophistication.
caption: Shadows, slopes, and circles are different faces of one turning structure.

source_key: A.6 Historical note: trigonometry was forced by the sky
title: Historical note: trigonometry was forced by the sky
role: Ground trig in astronomy, navigation, and surveying rather than textbook drills.
prompt_core: Create a night-to-dawn observational scene with astronomers, navigators, or surveyors using angular measurements to locate stars, distances, and directions. The circle of the sky and the practical need to project it into numbers should dominate the mood. Historical, luminous, not romanticized into fantasy.
caption: Trigonometry was born because the sky and the sea demanded it.

source_key: A.7 Why this interlude pays rent later
title: Why this interlude pays rent later
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
role: Bridge turning to complex numbers, waves, and calculus.
prompt_core: Design a visual bridge where a turning radius on a circle smoothly becomes an oscillating sine wave, a complex-plane rotation, and later a wave or differential-equation motif. This should feel like one idea changing clothes, not several disconnected applications. Elegant continuity, strong visual rhythm, future-facing.
caption: Turning becomes projection, oscillation, rotation, and eventually waves.
master_preamble: Create a visually sophisticated illustration for an adult mathematics book written for neurodivergent visual thinkers learning from first principles. Keep geometry believable, lighting restrained, and enough quiet negative space for a caption or later overlay.

title: Thought experiments
role: Invite the learner to physically feel angle, radian, and projection.
prompt_core: Show a person or arm turning through space, a wheel rolling a radius along its own arc, and a shadow length changing as a light rotates. Keep each mini-scene spare and physically believable. The goal is to make trig inhabit the body before it returns to symbols.
caption: Feel the turn, then watch its projections appear.
